acoustic_nodes.description: Temperature-dependent, peak above 65°F. Ground crickets (Gryllidae, 3,000-8,000 Hz, continuous pulse rate, Dolbear's Law temperature indicator). Katydids (Tettigoniidae, 6,000-15,000+ Hz, species-specific temporal patterning). Beetles and other stridulating insects (lower-amplitude continuous). Combined: spectrally broad (3-15+ kHz), rhythmically complex, from ground to mid-canopy.

Pacific Wren: 10g bird, 4-8 second complex song, up to 10 songs/minute at peak spring, understory to ground. Hermit Thrush: two-syrinx independent singing, spiraling phrases, ethereal quality, mid-canopy. Swainson's Thrush: ascending spiral, arrives April, multiple individuals. Chestnut-backed Chickadee: continuous contact calls all heights. Plus Steller's Jay, Varied Thrush, Song Sparrow, warblers, crow, raven. 10-15+ acoustic species simultaneously.

Rainforest big-drop effect: canopy redistributes droplets to larger size, deeper less-even texture than open rain. Primary surfaces: sword fern and bigleaf maple (large leaf, distinct percussion). Canopy drip post-rain: irregular musical drops, characteristic rainforest acoustic signature, persists long after rain stops. Hoh River tributary spring flood: maximum flow acoustic output (roar over cobble).

theory_nodes.concept: Simultaneous complexity at three levels: (1) Species richness: maximum acoustic species per Pacific Northwest temperate biome. (2) Multi-layer structure: each meta-layer (insect, bird, rain) is itself multiple simultaneous sub-layers at different frequencies, positions, and temporal patterns. (3) Full spectrum coverage: insects 3-15 kHz, birds 1-10 kHz, rain broadband, river 20-2,000 Hz. The full audible spectrum simultaneously occupied.
theory_nodes.description: Three-level simultaneous complexity: species richness + multi-layer structure + full spectrum coverage.

theory_nodes.concept: Pacific Wren (Troglodytes pacificus): arguably the most acoustically remarkable PNW rainforest bird. ~10g, song 4-8 seconds of continuous rapid multi-note phrases, up to 10 songs/minute at peak. Song volume extraordinary for body size. Sings from understory at close range -- immediate, surrounding, high-amplitude relative to body mass. The engine's closest acoustic equivalent to a bird that sings louder than its size justifies.
theory_nodes.description: Pacific Wren -- volume disproportionate to mass, the understory voice of the Hoh peak spring.

theory_nodes.concept: Hermit Thrush two-syrinx singing: the syrinx (avian vocal organ) has two independent sides that can produce different notes simultaneously. The Hermit Thrush uses this capability to produce harmonically complex songs that sound like a single voice but contain genuine polyphony. The ethereal quality of the Hermit Thrush song is a product of this anatomical capability. The song is literally multiple sounds produced simultaneously by a single bird.
theory_nodes.description: Hermit Thrush syrinx polyphony -- anatomical basis for the ethereal quality, genuine two-voice singing.

theory_nodes.concept: Hoh Rainforest: 12-14 feet annual precipitation, Olympic Peninsula western slope. Most productive temperate rainforest on continent. Multi-layered canopy to 300 feet. The acoustic environment is defined by this layered structure: sound absorbed, reflected, filtered at multiple height levels. The rainforest big-drop effect: overstory canopy redistributes rainfall droplets to larger size, producing deeper percussion on understory surfaces than direct rainfall would.
theory_nodes.description: Hoh Rainforest acoustics -- multi-layer canopy, big-drop effect, the physical basis for the soundscape.

cross_references.relationship: E=10 + E=10 = E=20. Simultaneous synthesis pairing. Accused crossover thrash = hardcore punk + thrash metal simultaneously. Hoh peak spring = insects + birds + rain simultaneously. Both about complexity through synthesis of multiple simultaneous distinct elements.

Both multi-species simultaneous convergence at E=10. 349 = fall river valley. 351 = spring rainforest. Same convergence structure: multiple distinct layers at simultaneous maximum. Different ecosystems, different seasons.

Both rainforest/riparian multi-species acoustic events. 332 = polyrhythm (three species in rhythmic relationship). 351 = biodiversity maximum (three meta-layers in simultaneous fullness). Both Hoh-proximate ecosystems.

s36_pair.pairing_note: E=10 + E=10 = E=20. Simultaneous synthesis at maximum. Accused crossover thrash: hardcore velocity + thrash architecture + extreme content simultaneously. Hoh biodiversity maximum: insects + birds + rain simultaneously. Both are about multiple distinct elements present simultaneously at their individual maximums, creating complexity that exceeds any individual component.
